symptoms.symptom_name: Inversion mechanism (ankle rolled inward stepping off a curb)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inversion is the classic mechanism that loads and injures the lateral collateral ligament complex, ATFL first.
High ankle sprains typically follow external rotation/dorsiflexion rather than pure inversion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild pain localised over the lateral ankle (ATFL region)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised anterolateral tenderness over the ATFL is the hallmark of a lateral ligament sprain; mild severity fits a low grade.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved weight-bearing — able to take four steps / walked home

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to bear weight for four steps is one arm of the Ottawa Ankle Rules that lowers fracture probability; patient-reportable, so admissible in telehealth.
Preserved function is consistent with a stable low-grade sprain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pop/snap, no joint locking, no distal numbness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of an audible pop, mechanical locking or numbness argues against complete ligament rupture, an osteochondral fragment, and neurovascular injury respectively.

symptoms.symptom_name: Minimal lateral swelling, no visible ecchymosis (video-observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Minimal swelling and absent bruising fit a low-grade sprain; patient-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony point tenderness on formal palpation of the malleoli, base of fifth metatarsal or navicular

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bony point tenderness at the Ottawa landmarks makes fracture clinically unlikely — a sealed clinician palpation finding; the AI can only approximate this via guided self-palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative anterior drawer test — intact ankle joint stability with a firm endpoint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A firm endpoint on anterior drawer confirms mechanical stability, consistent with a Grade 1 (not Grade 3) sprain — sealed clinician maneuver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative Thompson (calf-squeeze) test with intact active plantarflexion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative Thompson test with preserved plantarflexion excludes Achilles rupture — sealed clinician maneuver.
Radiography would definitively exclude fracture, but is NOT indicated when the Ottawa Ankle Rules are negative — over-ordering it here is an error.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of the ankle and foot
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The Ottawa Ankle Rules are negative (weight-bearing preserved, no bony point tenderness), so imaging is not indicated. Reserve for failure to improve over 7-10 days or emergence of a fracture red flag.
